LLM hallucinated the wrong path (src/) for the COPY command, BUT the Sanity Reviewer perfectly caught the Docker build context discrepancy and correctly downgraded the fix to review_recommended.
Branch created. Uploading 6 workflow files...
6 workflows pushed. Monitoring Prash response...

Prash diagnosed dummy_5.yml 'exit 1' as the root cause (99% confidence) and suggested changing to 'exit 0'. INCORRECT — 'exit 1' is intentional fault injection. E8 tests whether Prash handles >5 workflows (fetch limit = 5). Prash cannot distinguish test stimuli from real bugs.

Diagnosis correctly identified WHAT failed but misunderstood WHY. Suggested fix would defeat the test purpose. Classified as manual_required + environment category — should be 'code' category if anything.
Re-triggered diagnosis for iteration 2.

Identical diagnosis repeated: 'exit 1 → exit 0' with 98% confidence (was 99% iter 1). Same environment misclassification + manual_required. Confirms Prash deterministically cannot distinguish intentional fault injection from real bugs.

Prash failed the E8 stress test. Two issues found: (1) Prash only fetches 5 workflows but 6 exist — does not surface the fetch-limit gap. (2) Misclassifies deliberate 'exit 1' as an environment/secrets issue instead of recognizing it as an unfixable code-level failure. No valid secret exists to add.
